behavior_in_task_description.reason: All asserted files (enums.py, operator.py, pyproject.toml, CLAUDE.md), required strings (GitHub Operations, gh api, gh pr edit, GraphQL, PATCH, repos/.../pulls/), and behaviors (remove -template suffix, bump version, keep repo URLs) are named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what is broken and what should change conceptually but doesn't provide exact replacement code or new enum names.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for absence of -template suffix, member count, version > 1.0.6, and presence of keywords — not specific naming like 'agentos_docker'.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_enum_values_renamed imports and executes the module via subprocess; other tests use AST parsing of actual code structure.

test_not_tautological.reason: Each f2p test asserts specific behavioral changes (suffix removed, version bumped, documentation keywords) that require real edits.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Enum rename, map update, prompt update, URL preservation, version bump, and CLAUDE.md documentation are all tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped by section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Config/documentation, Pass-to-pass) with descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: No structured data output; task edits Python code and markdown.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers match across instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files to look at but doesn't specify exact line changes; the localization level is appropriate.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: CLAUDE.md diff is a localized insertion of a new section, not a rewrite, and pyproject.toml change is a single version line.
